result:
Eliezer Ben-Yehuda is widely recognized as the father of modern Hebrew[1](https://malkadrucker.com/eliezer-ben-yehudah-father-of-modern-hebrew/), [2](https://fozmuseum.com/blog/eliezer-ben-yehuda/), [3](https://jewishvirtuallibrary.org/ben-yehuda-eliezer). His birth name was Eliezer Yitzchak Perlman (or Perelman)[4](https://aish.com/eliezer-ben-yehuda-father-of-modern-day-hebrew/), [5](https://israeled.org/eliezer-ben-yehuda-is-born/), [6](https://engines.egr.uh.edu/episode/2882), [7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_Ben-Yehuda), [8](https://kids.kiddle.co/Eliezer_Ben-Yehuda), [9](https://www.myjewishlearning.com/article/eliezer-ben-yehuda/).

The father of Eliezer Ben-Yehuda, the father of modern Hebrew, was Yehuda Leib Perlman[7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliezer_Ben-Yehuda), [8](https://kids.kiddle.co/Eliezer_Ben-Yehuda).

direct_output:
<answer>
Process Summary:
1. The query asked for the identity of the father of the father of modern Hebrew.
2. Using the Ground_Google_Search_Tool, it was determined that Eliezer Ben-Yehuda is widely recognized as the father of modern Hebrew.
3. Further research identified that Eliezer Ben-Yehuda's birth name was Eliezer Yitzchak Perlman (or Perelman).
4. It was found that the father of Eliezer Ben-Yehuda was Yehuda Leib Perlman.

Answer: The father of the father of modern Hebrew is Yehuda Leib Perlman.
</answer>
